source: Worker 2 at 3.25.118.108 (instance shut down after completion)
methodology: Initial SSH data capture + local compiler testing + spec/stdlib cross-reference

failure_reason_breakdown.max_repairs_exceeded: ~95% of failures (all repair attempts exhausted after 5 iterations)

critical_infrastructure_bug.description: worker-generate.py line 243 uses 'tkc source.tk -o output' but tkc only supports '--out' (not '-o'). This means EVERY program that passes --check still fails the test phase with 'tkc: unknown flag: -o'. Many 'failures' are build infrastructure failures, not code quality issues.
critical_infrastructure_bug.evidence: error.log for AIA-001 says 'Build failed: tkc: unknown flag: -o'
critical_infrastructure_bug.fix: Change line 243 in worker-generate.py from '-o' to '--out'
critical_infrastructure_bug.impact: This alone could dramatically improve the compile+test pass rate. Programs that pass --check but fail -o are counted as failures and enter the repair loop, which then corrupts working code.

primary_causes: Using 'fn' instead of 'f=' for function declarations
Using 'for' instead of 'lp' for loops
Missing parentheses in if() conditions
Using '->' for return types instead of ':'
Using curly braces without toke delimiters
Using commas ',' as separators (toke uses ';')
Using square brackets '[' ']' (toke uses '@()')
Using '|' pipe character
Using '#' for comments (toke has no comment syntax in P1)
Using '?' ternary operator
Omitting semicolons between statements
Using 'while(cond){' instead of 'lp(;cond;){'
Cascading parse errors from other syntax mistakes
Using 'else {' instead of 'el{'
Calling stdlib functions that don't exist (e.g., math.sqrt, math.abs, math.pow)
Using variable names before declaration
Misspelling toke keywords or stdlib function names
Referencing types or modules not imported
Passing i64 where $str expected or vice versa
Missing 'as i64' or 'as u64' casts
Returning wrong type from function
Array element type inconsistencies
Mismatched braces from using wrong syntax patterns
Cascading from 'for(' parse failures
Missing closing ')' on @() array literals
Missing error union variants in mt (match) expressions
Not handling $err arm in result types
Incomplete sum type matching
Putting function declarations before module/import declarations
Interleaving imports with function definitions
Missing m= module declaration at top

top_10_error_codes_by_frequency.description: Wrong argument count in function call
primary_causes: Passing wrong number of args to stdlib functions
Confusing ';' argument separator with statement terminator
Importing non-existent modules (std.regex, std.collections, std.os, std.net, std.fmt)
Note: --check doesn't catch all unresolved imports; these surface at link time

top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.error: E1003 — comma is outside allowed character set
top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.frequency: Very high — LLMs default to comma-separated params

top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.error: E1003 — square brackets not allowed in default mode

top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.frequency: High — 'return' is universal in other languages

top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.error: E2002 — 'for' parsed as function call, 'let' unexpected in expression

top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.frequency: Medium — dot after mut is non-obvious
Medium — Go/Rust don't require parens around conditions

top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.error: Various parse errors (colon is type separator only in function params)
top_10_wrong_syntax_patterns.frequency: Medium — most languages use colon for type annotations

missing_stdlib_functions.note: These modules/functions are commonly called by generated programs but do NOT exist in toke stdlib. --check may pass but linking will fail, or programs use them incorrectly.

non_existent_modules.note: No math module exists. Basic arithmetic via operators only.
Only @() arrays and maps via @() syntax exist.
Use str.concat and str.buf/str.add for formatting.
std.io has println/print/readln/eprintln. File ops are in std.file.

wrong_function_names.note: No format strings in toke; use string builder pattern
No file existence check in stdlib

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add character set rules
system_prompt_improvements.detail: The prompt must explicitly state: NO commas, NO square brackets, NO curly quotes, NO hash/pound. Semicolons separate everything (params, args, array elements, statements).

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add complete keyword mapping
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Include explicit mapping table: fn/func -> f=, return -> <, for/while -> lp, else -> el, match -> mt, true/false -> true/false (same), let -> let (same), if -> if (same but requires parens)

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add declaration ordering rule
system_prompt_improvements.detail: File structure must be: m=module; then i=alias:path; then t=type defs; then f=functions. This ordering is enforced by the parser (E2001).

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add complete stdlib reference
system_prompt_improvements.detail: List ALL 14 stdlib modules with ALL function signatures. Explicitly state which modules do NOT exist (no std.math, no std.regex, no std.collections, no std.os, no std.net, no std.fmt, no std.sort).

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add mutable variable syntax
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Mutable binding: let x=mut.VALUE (dot required). Mutable array: let arr=mut.@(). Mutable integer: let n=mut.0. Reassignment: x=newval (no 'let').

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add array/map syntax examples
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Array literal: @(1;2;3). Map literal: @("key1":"val1";"key2":"val2"). Array methods: .get(i), .push(val), .len. Mutable array: let a=mut.@(); a=a.push(item).

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add error handling syntax
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Error union return: f=foo():$str!$err{...}. Return error: <$err("msg"). Match result: mt expr { $ok:v ...; $err:e ... }. Error propagation: expr!.

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add string builder pattern
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Show the str.buf()/str.add()/str.done() pattern explicitly as it replaces printf/format/sprintf in toke.

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add 3 complete working examples
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Include small (5-line), medium (20-line), and large (50-line) complete working programs as few-shot examples in the system prompt.

system_prompt_improvements.title: Add type system basics
system_prompt_improvements.detail: Primitive types: i64, u64, f64, bool, $str. User types: t=$typename{field:type;...}. Sum types: t=$typename{$variant1:type;$variant2:type}. Casting: (expr as target_type).

worker_script_fixes.title: Fix -o flag to --out
worker_script_fixes.detail: worker-generate.py line 243: change '-o' to '--out'. This single fix could recover a significant portion of 'failed' programs that actually compile correctly.

worker_script_fixes.title: Add error classification to failure records
worker_script_fixes.detail: Parse tkc JSON diagnostic output and record error_code, stage, and message in the failure metadata. This enables automated analysis without re-running.

worker_script_fixes.title: Separate compile failures from test failures
worker_script_fixes.detail: Track whether failure was at --check stage, --out build stage, or test execution stage. Currently all are lumped as 'max_repairs_exceeded'.

worker_script_fixes.title: Add error code to repair prompt
worker_script_fixes.detail: Include the specific error code (E2002, E1003, etc.) in the repair prompt so Claude can map errors to specific syntax rules.

compiler_error_message_improvements.title: Add fix suggestions for common LLM mistakes
compiler_error_message_improvements.detail: When E1003 fires for comma, suggest: 'use ; to separate parameters, arguments, and array elements'. When E1003 fires for [, suggest: 'use @() for arrays'. These already partially exist (good!) but could be more prescriptive.

compiler_error_message_improvements.title: Detect and suggest for 'for' loops
compiler_error_message_improvements.detail: When 'for' is used as an identifier followed by '(', emit W1020 suggesting 'lp(init;cond;step){body}' pattern. Currently 'for' isn't detected as a foreign keyword.

compiler_error_message_improvements.title: Add 'did you mean' for stdlib functions
compiler_error_message_improvements.detail: When an unresolved function is called (E3011), check against known stdlib function names and suggest the correct one (e.g., 'did you mean str.toint instead of str.parseInt?').

compiler_error_message_improvements.title: Better error for mut without dot
compiler_error_message_improvements.detail: E2002 'expected ., got @' could say: 'mutable bindings require a dot: mut.@() not mut@()'

language_simplification_opportunities.title: Consider allowing -o as alias for --out
language_simplification_opportunities.detail: Most compilers support -o. Adding this alias is trivial and matches universal convention.

language_simplification_opportunities.title: Consider str.join() stdlib addition
language_simplification_opportunities.detail: str.join(arr;sep) is extremely common. Currently requires manual str.buf() loop. Adding it would reduce code size and error surface.

language_simplification_opportunities.title: Consider std.math module
language_simplification_opportunities.detail: sqrt, abs, pow, floor, ceil, min, max are called in ~30% of attempted programs. A minimal math module would significantly reduce failures.

language_simplification_opportunities.title: The semicolon-everywhere design is the single biggest LLM friction point
language_simplification_opportunities.detail: Commas are deeply ingrained in LLM training data. Every generated program uses commas. This is by design (toke optimizes for token efficiency), but it means system prompts MUST heavily emphasize this rule.

current_prompt_weaknesses: Too vague — says 'use toke syntax' without defining what toke syntax is
No stdlib function list — model hallucinates functions
No keyword mapping — model uses fn/for/while/else/return
No character set rules — model uses commas and square brackets
No examples — no few-shot learning of correct patterns
Error is passed as raw text — should be parsed and categorized
recommended_repair_prompt_structure: 1. Explicit character set: only these characters are allowed: a-z A-Z 0-9 = ( ) { } ; : < > + - * / . @ $ ! " space newline
2. Keyword table: fn->f=, return-><, for/while->lp, else->el, match->mt
3. Separator rule: ALL separators are semicolons. NO commas anywhere.
5. The specific error code and its meaning
6. One correct example matching the error pattern
7. The complete list of available stdlib modules and functions

estimated_impact_of_fixes.fix_o_flag_bug: +20-30% pass rate (programs that compile but fail build)
estimated_impact_of_fixes.improved_system_prompt: +25-35% pass rate (fewer syntax errors on first generation)
estimated_impact_of_fixes.combined_estimate: Could raise 13% to 60-70% compile+test pass rate
